input.command:
rm -f /tmp/qemu-monitor.sock
nohup qemu-system-i386 \
  -M pc \
  -cpu pentium \
  -m 32 \
  -hda /app/isos/win311.img \
  -snapshot \
  -vga std \
  -vnc :1 \
  -monitor unix:/tmp/qemu-monitor.sock,server,nowait \
  -k en-us \
  -boot c \
  -rtc base=localtime \
  >/tmp/qemu.log 2>&1 &
echo "PID=$!"
sleep 5
echo "---log---"; tail -20 /tmp/qemu.log
echo "---procs---"; ps aux | grep qemu-system | grep -v grep | head
echo "---ports---"; ss -tlnp | grep -E ':5901'
echo "---sock---"; ls -la /tmp/qemu-monitor.sock

content.text: QEMU is up. Now let me verify it boots to the Windows desktop via a monitor screendump, and set up nginx + noVNC on port 80.

input.command:
which socat || apt-get install -y socat >/tmp/socat.log 2>&1; which socat
# helper to send monitor command
echo "screendump /tmp/screen.ppm" | socat - unix-connect:/tmp/qemu-monitor.sock
sleep 1
ls -la /tmp/screen.ppm; file /tmp/screen.ppm 2>&1 | head -1

input.command:
# Configure nginx to serve noVNC on port 80, proxying websocket to VNC 5901.
# Start websockify bridging 6080 -> 5901 (localhost)
nohup websockify --web=/usr/share/novnc 6080 localhost:5901 >/tmp/websockify.log 2>&1 &
echo "WS_PID=$!"
sleep 2
tail -5 /tmp/websockify.log
ss -tlnp | grep 6080

content.text:
WS_PID=6325
WebSocket server settings:
  - Listen on :6080
  - Web server. Web root: /usr/share/novnc
  - No SSL/TLS support (no cert file)
  - proxying from :6080 to localhost:5901
LISTEN 0      100          0.0.0.0:6080       0.0.0.0:*    users:(("websockify",pid=6325,fd=3))

input.command:
cp /app/novnc.nginx.conf /etc/nginx/sites-available/novnc
ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/novnc /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/novnc
rm -f /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default
nginx -t 2>&1
nginx 2>&1 || service nginx start 2>&1 || nginx -g 'daemon on;' 2>&1
sleep 1
ss -tlnp | grep -E ':80\b'
echo "---http test---"
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "root:%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1/vnc.html
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "lite:%{http_code}\n" http://127.0.0.1/vnc_lite.html
